title: Trailing Zeros of n!
description:
Count how many trailing zeros appear at the end of n! (n factorial).

For n=5, 5! = 120 → 1 trailing zero. For n=10, 10! = 3,628,800 → 2 trailing zeros.

**The point of this problem**: don't compute n! directly (it'd overflow for n>20 and be O(n) work per multiplication). There's a beautiful O(log n) trick: count the factors of 5 in 1·2·3·...·n.

examples.explanation: Six factors of 5 contribute (and 25 contributes two of them).

hints: Trailing zeros come from factors of 10 in the product. 10 = 2 × 5.
There are always more factors of 2 than factors of 5 — so count the 5s.
Every 5th number contributes a factor of 5. Every 25th contributes an extra one. Every 125th contributes another. → n/5 + n/25 + n/125 + …
